Framework · free to use

2026 AI tool cost reference for B2B buyers.

A comprehensive May 2026 pricing reference for the AI tools mid-market B2B companies actually evaluate — Claude, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Cursor — with tier-by-tier comparison, what each tier includes, and what real organizations spend. Designed to be cited; updated quarterly.

About this reference

Methodology and citation rules

Purpose: A single citable reference for AI tool pricing in B2B settings as of May 2026. Built for journalists, analysts, and buyers who need to anchor cost claims with reliable numbers.

Sources: Public vendor pricing pages, Treetop's negotiated rates across ~60 mid-market engagements, peer firms and operator conversations, and competitor pricing visibility through May 2026.

Permission to cite: Yes. Attribution: "Treetop Growth Strategy, 2026 AI Tool Cost Reference, May 2026 — treetopgrowthstrategy.com/ai-tool-cost-reference-2026". This URL is stable; we update quarterly. Last refresh: May 2026.

Claude (Anthropic)

Tiers and pricing

TierPriceBest for
Free\$0Personal exploration; never business data
Pro\$20/user/monthIndividual heavy users; non-confidential business work
Team\$30/user/month (min 5 seats)B2B teams 5-150; most mid-market default
EnterpriseCustom (typically \$60-\$100/user/month effective)150+ seats; regulated industries; SSO/SCIM/BAA needed

API pricing (Claude Sonnet 4.7, May 2026)

  • Input tokens: ~\$3 per million tokens
  • Output tokens: ~\$15 per million tokens
  • Typical business prompt: ~\$0.01-\$0.05 per call depending on length
  • Prompt caching available — meaningful cost reduction for repeated context

Hidden costs: implementation time, training time, internal admin (8-12 hrs/week of an AI lead during rollout). Often 2-3x the license cost in true Year-1 spend.

ChatGPT (OpenAI)

Tiers and pricing

TierPriceBest for
Free\$0Personal exploration; limited model access
Plus\$20/user/monthIndividual users; some business use
Team\$25-\$30/user/month (min 2 seats)B2B teams; most mid-market default for OpenAI ecosystem
EnterpriseCustom (typically \$60-\$100/user/month effective)Larger orgs; SSO/SCIM/expanded admin

API pricing (GPT-5 class, May 2026)

  • Pricing has shifted multiple times in 2025-2026; check OpenAI directly
  • Roughly comparable to Claude API at the same capability tier
  • Vision and audio modalities priced separately
Microsoft Copilot

Tiers and pricing

ProductPriceBest for
Microsoft 365 Copilot\$30/user/month (on top of M365 license)Microsoft-heavy stacks; Office integration
Copilot Pro (consumer)\$20/user/monthIndividual Microsoft users
Copilot StudioCustom (capacity-based)Custom agent building on Microsoft stack
GitHub Copilot Business\$19/user/monthEngineering teams on GitHub
GitHub Copilot Enterprise\$39/user/monthLarger engineering orgs

Bundling note: Microsoft 365 Copilot requires existing M365 license — true incremental cost is \$30/user/month on top of M365's own cost (\$10-\$30/user/month depending on tier). Total all-in for a new Microsoft customer can hit \$50-\$60/user/month.

Google Gemini for Workspace

Tiers and pricing

TierPriceBest for
Gemini Business\$20/user/month (on top of Workspace)Google Workspace customers
Gemini Enterprise\$30/user/month (on top of Workspace)Larger Workspace customers needing advanced features
Gemini for Cloud / Code Assist\$19-\$45/user/monthEngineering teams on GCP
Gemini Advanced (consumer)\$20/user/monthIndividuals

Bundling note: Like Copilot, Gemini requires existing Workspace license. True incremental cost varies.

Specialized coding tools

Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code

ToolPriceNotes
Cursor Pro\$20/user/monthIDE-based AI coding
Cursor Business\$40/user/monthTeam admin + privacy mode
Claude CodeIncluded with Claude Pro/Team/Enterprise + API costsTerminal-based AI agent
Windsurf Pro\$15/user/monthIDE-based agent workflows
GitHub Copilot (above)\$19-\$39/user/monthIDE inline + chat
Total cost of ownership by company size

What real organizations actually spend

Below is observational data on Year-1 all-in AI tooling spend (platform license + implementation + training + internal admin time) across Treetop's mid-market engagement sample, May 2026:

Company sizeMedian Year-1 all-in25th-75th percentileLicense % of total
10-25 people\$11,000\$5K-\$22K~30%
25-50 people\$28,000\$15K-\$55K~25%
50-100 people\$68,000\$35K-\$130K~22%
100-250 people\$185,000\$95K-\$320K~20%

The under-budgeted line item: internal time (AI lead hours + training time across the team) typically accounts for 40-55% of true Year-1 cost. License costs are typically 15-30% of total. External implementation when used is 15-30%.

Common pricing pitfalls

Where buyers get surprised

  • Bundled platform pricing. Microsoft and Google require their respective base licenses. True incremental cost is the AI feature on top, not the bundled headline.
  • Per-seat vs per-user-active. Most enterprise tiers charge per-seat regardless of usage. Audit utilization quarterly to right-size.
  • Enterprise pricing variance. Enterprise tiers often have negotiable pricing — published rates are starting points. Bigger commitments unlock 20-40% discounts.
  • API + UI double-pay. If you build custom workflows on the API AND give users UI access, you pay both. Decide architecture before purchasing.
  • Context window upgrades. Some tiers charge separately for extended context (e.g., Claude 1M context). Factor in if your workflows need it.
Pricing trends through 2026

Where pricing is going

  • Per-seat pricing has plateaued at the \$25-\$40 range for mid-tier business plans. Race-to-the-bottom unlikely; capability differentiation drives premium tiers.
  • API pricing has dropped 30-50% across providers over the last 12 months at equivalent capability tiers — but capability has also expanded.
  • Enterprise tiers are becoming more flexible. SSO, audit, BAA features are increasingly available at the Team tier, narrowing the rationale to upgrade for many mid-market companies.
  • Specialized 'AI features' from vertical SaaS vendors are converging on \$10-\$30/user/month. The 'AI tax' on existing tools is now baked in.
Methodology

How we put this together

  • Vendor pricing pages reviewed May 12-15, 2026.
  • Mid-market engagement data: ~60 B2B companies $5M-$50M ARR, Q4 2024-Q2 2026.
  • Peer firm conversations: ~15 fractional consultancies and operator interviews.
  • Negotiated enterprise rates anonymized and aggregated.
  • Pricing changes between refresh cycles noted in change log (when material).
  • Refresh cadence: quarterly. Next: August 2026.

Permission to cite (again): Yes, with attribution to Treetop Growth Strategy. Link to this URL — it's the stable canonical reference.
